root_text.an5.127:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu nālaṁ saṅghamhā vapakāsituṁ.
root_text.an5.127:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu asantuṭṭho hoti itarītarena cīvarena, asantuṭṭho hoti itarītarena piṇḍapātena, asantuṭṭho hoti itarītarena senāsanena, asantuṭṭho hoti itarītarena gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārena, kāmasaṅkappabahulo ca viharati.
root_text.an5.127:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu nālaṁ saṅghamhā vapakāsituṁ.
root_text.an5.127:2.1: Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ saṅghamhā vapakāsituṁ.
root_text.an5.127:2.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu santuṭṭho hoti itarītarena cīvarena, santuṭṭho hoti itarītarena piṇḍapātena, santuṭṭho hoti itarītarena senāsanena, santuṭṭho hoti itarītarena gilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārena, nekkhammasaṅkappabahulo ca viharati.
root_text.an5.127:2.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu alaṁ saṅghamhā vapakāsitun”ti.

translation_text.an5.127:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with five qualities is not fit to live apart from a Saṅgha community.
translation_text.an5.127:1.3: It’s when a mendicant is not content with any kind of robe, almsfood, lodging, and medicines and supplies for the sick. And they have a lot of sensual thoughts.
translation_text.an5.127:1.4: A mendicant with these five qualities is not fit to live apart from a Saṅgha community.
translation_text.an5.127:2.1: A mendicant with five qualities is fit to live apart from a Saṅgha community.
translation_text.an5.127:2.3: It’s when a mendicant is content with any kind of robe, almsfood, lodging, and medicines and supplies for the sick. And they think a lot about renunciation.
translation_text.an5.127:2.4: A mendicant with these five qualities is fit to live apart from a Saṅgha community.”
